description: Post-hoc noise-floor diagnostics for R9-DAG dose-direction stability test
single_replicate_note: 80% of dose-level groups (drug x cell x dose) contain exactly one replicate profile. The mean signature for these groups is a single z-score vector, and two single-replicate z-score vectors in 978 dimensions have expected cosine near zero regardless of the true underlying direction.

near_identical_dose_diagnostic.description: Pairs where dose ratio < 1.01 (e.g., 9.99 vs 10.00 uM — same intended dose with measurement imprecision). These should have cosine near 1.0 if directions are stable, or near 0 if dominated by noise.
near_identical_dose_diagnostic.interpretation: Median cosine of 0.11 establishes the noise floor. The overall median of 0.19 is barely above it, confirming that single-replicate noise dominates the test.

5_to_15_uM.description: Standard pharmacological dose range (includes 10 uM, the modal LINCS dose)
5_to_15_uM.note: Worst performance at the standard dose range — consistent with these being dominated by near-identical doses where noise floor is most apparent

conclusion: The pre-registered R9-DAG criteria cannot be met at this replicate depth. The test lacks statistical power to distinguish genuine dose-dependent direction changes from single-replicate noise in 978 dimensions. A properly powered test would require >= 3 replicates per dose level.
